title: Thermal Stability of IgG4 Monoclonal Antibodies: Ambiguous Roles of Co-Solutes, Excipients and Salts in Conformational and Colloidal States
author: Ji Young Yang, Oliver Burkert, Boris Mizaikoff, Jens Smiatek
abstract: Mechanistic study of arginine and NaCl effects on antibody stability, revealing dual roles in unfolding and aggregation suppression. Integrated experimental and Kirkwood-Buff theoretical framework.

title: Influence of Co-Solutes and Solvents on Diffusion Interaction Parameters in Multicomponent Solutions: New Insights through the Kirkwood--Buff Theory
author: Jiyoung Yang, Matthias Brosz, Niklas Adebar, Oliver Burkert, Moritz Schulze, Jens Smiatek
abstract: Development of theoretical framework for understanding diffusion interactions in pharmaceutical formulations using Kirkwood-Buff theory, providing mechanistic insights for drug formulation design.

title: Multidomain Protein--Urea Interactions: Differences in Binding Behavior Lead to Different Destabilization Tendencies for Monoclonal Antibodies
author: Ji Young Yang, Oliver Burkert, Boris Mizaikoff, Jens Smiatek
abstract: Comprehensive molecular dynamics study revealing domain-specific urea binding to antibodies and their differential destabilization mechanisms, crucial for understanding protein stability in formulations.

title: Impact of Urea on Monoclonal Antibodies: Multiple Destabilization and Aggregation Effects for Therapeutic Immunoglobulin G Proteins
author: Ji Young Yang, Oliver Burkert, Boris Mizaikoff, Jens Smiatek
abstract: Investigation of multiple destabilization pathways induced by urea in therapeutic antibodies, providing insights for formulation development and quality control.

title: Combination of Explainable Machine Learning and Conceptual Density Functional Theory: Applications for the Study of Key Solvation Mechanisms
author: I-Ting Ho, Milena Matysik, Liliana Montano Herrera, Jiyoung Yang, Ralph Joachim Guderlei, Michael Laussegger, Bernhard Schrantz, Regine Hammer, Ramón Alain Miranda-Quintana, Jens Smiatek
abstract: Integration of explainable AI with conceptual DFT for understanding solvation mechanisms, demonstrating the power of interpretable machine learning in chemistry.

title: Artificial Neural Networks for the Prediction of Solvation Energies Based on Experimental and Computational Data
author: Jiyoung Yang, Matthias J. Knape, Oliver Burkert, Virginia Mazzini, Alexander Jung, Vincent S. J. Craig, Ramón Alain Miranda-Quintana, Erich Bluhmki, Jens Smiatek
abstract: Novel neural network approach for predicting solvation energies from molecular properties, validated on both experimental and computational datasets, accelerating formulation screening.
